QF.id: XIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_61

question.text: How does the settlement of equity stock futures differ from equity index futures in India?

answer.text: Stock futures are physically settled (actual delivery of shares) since October 2019; index futures are cash settled (payment of price difference in INR)
Both require physical delivery of the underlying
Stock futures are cash settled; index futures require delivery of the constituent stocks

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Since October 2019, equity stock futures in India are physically settled — the long must take delivery of shares and the short must deliver them. Index futures remain cash settled because delivering a basket of stocks in exact index proportions is impractical. This is a SEBI mandate to reduce speculation in individual stock derivatives. **The Trap:** Before 2019, both were cash settled. The change to physical settlement for stock F&O was a significant market structure change. You can't physically deliver an index. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch7 covers this change. Currency futures (Series I) are cash settled. Commodity futures (Series XVI) can be both. This comparison is key for Series XIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Physical settlement means you need the stock in your demat account (if short) or sufficient funds (if long) at expiry — plan exits before the last few days to avoid delivery obligations you can't meet.
